landing.heroTitle: Calm, Guided Care for Your Pregnancy
landing.heroSubtitle: NURTURA helps you understand your symptoms, monitor your health, and stay connected to your care team. Safe, private, and always available.
featuresDetails: Pick symptoms, severity, and duration to avoid ambiguity.
Clear green/yellow/red outcomes with next steps.
Empathy + explanation + action, always with disclaimer.
Capture symptoms quickly and keep a safe timeline for your provider.
No free text. Structured, consistent, and clinically responsible.
Empathy + explanation + action, always with a medical disclaimer.
Low-connectivity fallback uses local safety rules.
English, Hindi, and Tamil with safe templates.
High-risk flows surface emergency actions instantly.

tipYouAreNotAlone: You are not alone. We are here to help.

green.label: Doing well
green.title: You are doing well today
green.body: Keep tracking how you feel. We are here when you need extra support.

yellow.label: Monitor
yellow.title: Take a calm check-in
yellow.body: Monitor symptoms and re-check later today. Reach out if anything worsens.

red.label: High attention
red.title: We are here with you
red.body: Your recent signals suggest urgent attention. Please contact your provider or emergency services.

tips: Drink water regularly and keep small snacks nearby to avoid nausea.
Try gentle stretching or a short walk to ease tension.
Keep a simple symptom timeline to share with your provider.
Rest on your left side to support circulation.
If something feels off, reach out early—your care team is there for you.

landing.heroTitle: Calm guidance for pregnancy, right when it matters most.
landing.heroSubtitle: NURTURA helps you capture symptoms, understand risk signals, and feel supported between doctor visits.
landing.decisionSupportNotDiagnosis: NURTURA never diagnoses. It supports decision-making and always recommends consulting a healthcare provider for clinical care.

emergency.title: Emergency Alert
immediateActions: Call your doctor or midwife immediately
Prepare to go to hospital if instructed
emergency.breathingPattern: 4 seconds in → 4 seconds hold → 4 seconds out
emergency.locationUnavailable: Location not available. Please call emergency services.
emergency.shareText: Please help me. Here is my location.
emergency.shareFailed: Unable to share location right now.

GREEN: Rest, hydrate, and follow your care plan.
YELLOW: Monitor symptoms and re-check in 2–4 hours.
Contact your provider if symptoms persist or worsen.
Log any new symptoms or BP readings.
RED: Seek medical attention now (call emergency services if needed).
Do not drive yourself if you feel unwell—ask someone to accompany you.
Bring your symptom timeline and any BP readings.

headache.title: Headache
headache.recommendation: Rest in a quiet, dark room. Stay hydrated. Contact provider if persistent or with vision changes.

swelling.title: Swelling
swelling.recommendation: Elevate feet, reduce salt. Monitor BP if possible. Call provider if sudden or severe.

dizziness.title: Dizziness
dizziness.recommendation: Sit/lie down immediately. Hydrate. Seek help if fainting or chest pain.

bleeding.title: Bleeding
bleeding.recommendation: Call emergency services immediately. Lie on left side.

chest_pain.title: Chest Pain
chest_pain.recommendation: Call emergency services now. Stay calm.

shortness_breath.title: Shortness of Breath
shortness_breath.recommendation: Sit upright, loosen clothing. Call emergency if worsening.

blurred_vision.title: Blurred Vision
blurred_vision.recommendation: Emergency - possible preeclampsia. Call provider now.

hypertension_preeclampsia_risk.title: Hypertension risk
hypertension_preeclampsia_risk.recommendation: Recheck blood pressure and contact your provider if it stays at or above 140/90.

preeclampsia_possible.title: Possible preeclampsia pattern
preeclampsia_possible.recommendation: Headache with swelling (and high BP if present) can be serious after 20 weeks. Seek same-day clinical advice.

chronic_hypertension.title: Chronic hypertension history
chronic_hypertension.recommendation: Keep regular prenatal visits and discuss blood pressure targets.

gestational_diabetes_risk.title: Possible gestational diabetes risk
gestational_diabetes_risk.recommendation: Ask about glucose screening and diet guidance.

recurrent_preeclampsia_risk.title: Higher preeclampsia risk history
recurrent_preeclampsia_risk.recommendation: Because of your history, closer monitoring may be needed.

gestational_hypertension_possible.title: Possible gestational hypertension
gestational_hypertension_possible.recommendation: Monitor symptoms and contact your provider if they persist or worsen.

anemia_possible.title: Possible anemia pattern
anemia_possible.recommendation: Rest, hydrate, and discuss iron screening with your provider.

emergency.title: Emergency
emergency.recommendation: Seek immediate medical attention now. Call emergency services or go to the nearest hospital.
